Content: Schafer: I need to get accustomed to money being around.
Content_zh: Schafer：我得習慣身邊有錢的感覺。

Content: That's the thing.
Content_zh: 就是這樣。

Content: This is for the therapy, the physical therapy of getting me used to actually not being afraid of money.
Content_zh: 這是治療，讓我從身體上習慣真的不怕錢。

Content: Offscreen voice: Richard Branson does the same thing.
Content_zh: 畫外音：Richard Branson 也這樣做。

Content: Schafer: Ah! You guys, what is this stuff that's all over me?
Content_zh: Schafer：啊！你們幾個，這些沾滿我全身的是什麼東西？

Content: Offscreen voice: Beautiful.
Content_zh: 畫外音：漂亮。

Content: Schafer: I can't apply it to myself..
Content_zh: Schafer：我沒辦法自己塗……

Content: Offscreen voice: Oh my God.
Content_zh: 畫外音：天啊。

Content: Schafer: I can't self-terminate.
Content_zh: Schafer：我沒辦法自我終結。

Content: Offscreen voice: Help me apply money.
Content_zh: 畫外音：幫我塗錢。

Content: Schafer: I can't self-monetize.
Content_zh: Schafer：我沒辦法自我變現。

Content: Schafer: So this is a more recent, uh point-and-click adventure game.
Content_zh: Schafer：這是一款比較新的，呃，點擊式冒險遊戲。

Content: I have to show you someone else's, because I haven't made one in a long time.
Content_zh: 我得拿別人的給你們看，因為我很久沒做這類遊戲了。

Content: I used to make them back in the '90s.
Content_zh: 我以前 90 年代做過這種。

Content: This is Machinarium.
Content_zh: 這是《Machinarium》。

Content: You role play this little robot and you click around.
Content_zh: 你扮演這個小機器人，到處點來點去。

Content: And these kind of games, you walk the character around by clicking on the ground somewhere and pull on this handle here.
Content_zh: 這類遊戲就是點地上的某個地方讓角色走過去，再拉這裡的把手。

Content: And then I can make this mine cart come through the room.
Content_zh: 然後我就能讓這台礦車穿過房間。

Content: Shahani: I'm sorry. What's that?
Content_zh: Shahani：不好意思，那是什麼？

Content: Schafer: I pulled this handle, oh, that's right.
Content_zh: Schafer：我拉了這個把手，喔，對。

Content: This doesn't work until I do this.
Content_zh: 要先這樣做，這個才會動。

Content: That's what a lot of adventure game playing is like.
Content_zh: 很多冒險遊戲玩起來就是這樣。

Content: You're like, oh, this doesn't, why doesn't this work?
Content_zh: 你會想，喔，這不行，為什麼不能用？

Content: Oh, I need this gear first.
Content_zh: 喔，我得先拿到這個齒輪。

Content: I need to get the gear on the sprocket.
Content_zh: 我得把齒輪裝到鏈輪上。

Content: And so they're, they move at a slower pace.
Content_zh: 所以這類遊戲的節奏比較慢。

Content: And some people really like that.
Content_zh: 有些人很喜歡這樣。

Content: But it also means they're not exactly as attention-getting and as visceral and maybe as a big block buster action game.
Content_zh: 但這也表示，它們不像大型賣座動作遊戲那麼抓人、那麼直覺刺激。

Content: And it led us to this point now where it's really hard to get one of these games made these days,
Content_zh: 所以到了今天，要做出這類遊戲真的很難，

Content: or at least get funding for it from a publisher.
Content_zh: 至少很難從發行商那裡拿到資金。

Content: Shahani: Before getting into all that, what put you on the map?
Content_zh: Shahani：在談那些之前，是什麼讓你成名的？

Content: Like, just describe the game that got you into game famous.
Content_zh: 就是描述一下讓你在遊戲圈出名的那款遊戲。

Content: Schafer: Game famous.
Content_zh: Schafer：遊戲圈出名。

Content: You know, we just, each game we made in the '90s, we got a little more recognition for it.
Content_zh: 你知道，我們 90 年代每做一款遊戲，名氣就多一點。

Content: And we were always pretty savvy about getting our names on the box, you know, so we're, hey.
Content_zh: 而且我們一直很懂得把自己的名字放到盒子上，你知道，就像，嘿。

Content: You know, you have to kind of act like you're famous before you're famous.
Content_zh: 你知道，在真的出名前，你得先表現得像自己很紅。

Content: That sounds funny.
Content_zh: 這聽起來很好笑。

Content: I learned that from an interview with David Lee Roth about Van Halen.
Content_zh: 這是我從 David Lee Roth 談 Van Halen 的訪談裡學到的。

Content: Like we always acted like we were hot shit.
Content_zh: 就像我們一直裝得自己很了不起。

Content: We had a big hit, Full Throttle, it's called Full Throttle and it sold a million copies
Content_zh: 我們有一款大賣的遊戲《Full Throttle》，它賣了一百萬套，

Content: and that was really big for those PC graphic adventures.
Content_zh: 對當時的 PC 圖像冒險遊戲來說非常厲害。

Content: And then I made a game called Grim Fandango and that won Game of the Year with Game Spot
Content_zh: 後來我做了一款叫《Grim Fandango》的遊戲，拿了 GameSpot 的年度遊戲，

Content: and that was the big thing that helped a lot.
Content_zh: 那件事幫了很大的忙。

Content: Grim Fandango is set in the Land of the Dead, yeah.
Content_zh: 《Grim Fandango》的背景是亡者之地，對。

Content: And it was, it sold about half a million units and it was seen as not being commercially successful.
Content_zh: 它賣了大約五十萬套，但被認為商業上不算成功。

Content: And it was one of the reasons that adventure games died.
Content_zh: 這也是冒險遊戲沒落的原因之一。

Content: I mean, I heard a friend of mine, who worked in another game company, saying we tried to get an adventure game made
Content_zh: 我是說，我聽一個在別家遊戲公司工作的朋友說，他們想做一款冒險遊戲，

Content: and they said, well look, you know, Grim Fandango didn't do that well.
Content_zh: 結果對方說，你看，《Grim Fandango》賣得也沒那麼好。

Content: So obviously, you know, it was a good game, it won Game of the Year.
Content_zh: 所以很明顯，那是款好遊戲，還拿了年度遊戲。

Content: And if Game of the Year can't sell, you know, as many copies as it needs to, then maybe that genre's dead.
Content_zh: 如果年度遊戲都賣不到該有的數量，那也許這個類型已經死了。

Content: So I feel some responsibility to make a good game and make it do well.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得自己有責任做出一款好遊戲，還要讓它賣得好。

Content: Rice: He's been excited about it.
Content_zh: Rice：他一直對這件事很興奮。

Content: I mean, he's wanted to return to adventure games for a while and it's just been a matter of not knowing how to make that happen.
Content_zh: 我是說，他想回去做冒險遊戲已經一陣子了，只是不知道該怎麼成事。

Content: Schafer: But fans are always saying they would want, they want one, but a publisher would say, we can't make money doing those.
Content_zh: Schafer：可是粉絲總是說他們想要，想要一款，但發行商會說，我們做那種遊戲賺不到錢。

Content: So something like Kickstarter shows up, which is this crowd sourcing website, where everybody can back a project.
Content_zh: 然後 Kickstarter 這種群眾募資網站出現了，每個人都可以贊助一個專案。

Content: You pitch your project and you get backers and then if you meet your goals, you get the money and you build your project.
Content_zh: 你提出專案，找到贊助者，如果達成目標，就拿到錢去做專案。

Content: Doing an adventure game is interesting, because it's the only, like the only way you could do that,
Content_zh: 做冒險遊戲很有意思，因為這大概是唯一能這麼做的方式，

Content: letting the fans like, kind of put their money where their mouth is.
Content_zh: 讓粉絲真的掏錢證明自己說到做到。

Content: Is it that? Wait a second.
Content_zh: 是這樣講嗎？等一下。

Content: Did I get that backwards? Yeah.
Content_zh: 我是不是講反了？對。

Content: Host: No, you're right. Yeah.
Content_zh: 主持人：沒有，你講對了。對。

Content: Some loud mouth fan, no, I don't want to buy Trenched, but what about an adventure game?
Content_zh: 某個大嘴巴粉絲說，不，我不想買《Trenched》，冒險遊戲呢？

Content: Schafer: Exactly.
Content_zh: Schafer：沒錯。

Content: Shahani: Explain your Kickstarter campaign.
Content_zh: Shahani：說明一下你的 Kickstarter 募資專案。

Content: What have you just done?
Content_zh: 你們剛做了什麼？

Content: Schafer: Yeah. What have we done?
Content_zh: Schafer：對。我們做了什麼？

Content: Schafer: Oh, sorry. I didn't see you there.
Content_zh: Schafer：喔，抱歉。我沒看到你在那裡。

Content: You caught me indulging in one of my many impressive hobbies.
Content_zh: 你撞見我正在享受眾多厲害嗜好之一。

Content: I'm Tim Schafer.
Content_zh: 我是 Tim Schafer。

Content: Schafer: And so we put a pitch video up where we said we want to make a game.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以我們放了一支提案影片，說我們想做一款遊戲。

Content: And the idea was, what if we made one of these adventure games that most publishers don't want to fund these days?
Content_zh: 想法是，如果我們做一款現在多數發行商不想出錢的冒險遊戲，會怎麼樣？

Content: And one of my favorite types of games to make is adventure games.
Content_zh: 而冒險遊戲是我最喜歡做的遊戲類型之一。

Content: But these days, it seems like adventure games are almost a bit of a lost art form.
Content_zh: 可是這年頭，冒險遊戲幾乎像是一種失傳的藝術。

Content: They exist in our dreams and our memories and in Germany.
Content_zh: 它們存在於我們的夢裡、記憶裡，還有德國。

Content: Schafer: Maybe that would get people excited about getting a chance to do something that couldn't be funded any other way.
Content_zh: Schafer：也許這會讓大家興奮，因為有機會做一件用其他方式拿不到資金的事。

Content: Schafer: It's perfect.
Content_zh: Schafer：太完美了。

Content: We got the perfect team here at Double Fine to make it.
Content_zh: Double Fine 這裡有最適合做這件事的團隊。

Content: We got the inventor of the genre here, Ron Gilbert.
Content_zh: 我們這裡還有這個類型的發明者 Ron Gilbert。

Content: Oh, look! There he is right now.
Content_zh: 喔，你看！他剛好在那裡。

Content: Maybe he'll help us.
Content_zh: 也許他會幫我們。

Content: He'll help us.
Content_zh: 他會幫我們的。

Content: Gilbert: You know, we've always talked about wanting to do, you know, more classic adventure stuff.
Content_zh: Gilbert：你知道，我們一直在聊，想做更多經典冒險遊戲的東西。

Content: And so doing the Kickstarter thing, it's like, yeah, that sounds, I mean, that sounds really like an interesting idea.
Content_zh: 所以做 Kickstarter 這件事，就覺得，對，聽起來，我是說，聽起來真的很有趣。

Content: I think we were both very skeptical about the amount of money.
Content_zh: 我想我們兩個都很懷疑能籌到那麼多錢。

Content: Schafer: So, we are asking for this much to make the game and this much to film it.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以，我們要這麼多錢做遊戲，這麼多錢拍紀錄片。

Content: And that may seem like a lot of money, but it's really not that much for a game budget these days.
Content_zh: 這看起來也許很多，但以現在的遊戲預算來說真的不算多。

Content: It's pretty small, actually.
Content_zh: 其實還滿小的。

Content: Schafer: And I just never thought you could get the kind of money that would actually pay for a whole game budget.
Content_zh: Schafer：我從沒想過真的能籌到足以支付整款遊戲預算的錢。

Content: Schafer: Hello, potential backers.
Content_zh: Schafer：各位潛在贊助者，大家好。

Content: I could do all three options.
Content_zh: 三個版本我都可以演。

Content: Thank you for donating well over the amount we asked for.
Content_zh: 謝謝你們捐了遠超過我們要求的金額。

Content: That's so great.
Content_zh: 真的太棒了。

Content: We have too much money now.
Content_zh: 我們現在錢太多了。

Content: Damn you!
Content_zh: 可惡啊你們！

Content: Why haven't anyone given us any money?
Content_zh: 為什麼都沒有人給我們錢？

Content: I'm so sad.
Content_zh: 我好難過。

Content: That doesn't seem believable.
Content_zh: 這聽起來不太可信。

Content: Yeah, we're not going to use that one.
Content_zh: 對，我們不會用那個。

Content: Or just moderately, hello.
Content_zh: 或是普通一點，哈囉。

Content: Thank you for dedicating the appropriate amount of...
Content_zh: 謝謝你們投入適當數量的……

Content: Schafer: Because the previous projects in Kickstarter had only been, I don't know what the maximum was before then,
Content_zh: Schafer：因為 Kickstarter 之前的專案只有，我不知道當時最高是多少，

Content: but it looked like they're like forty grand.
Content_zh: 但看起來大概是四萬美元。

Content: You know, I mean, we're used to making projects that were like, you know, Brütal.
Content_zh: 你知道，我們習慣做的專案像是《Brütal Legend》。

Content: Like 20 million dollars and these smaller games, like two million.
Content_zh: 那種兩千萬美元的，還有比較小的遊戲，大概兩百萬。

Content: It was like, no way you can get two million from Kickstarter.
Content_zh: 所以就覺得，不可能從 Kickstarter 籌到兩百萬吧。

Content: Could you?
Content_zh: 可以嗎？

Content: Schafer: I hope this works out, because it is kind of, that enough people got together,
Content_zh: Schafer：我希望這會成功，因為某種程度上，如果有夠多人聚在一起，

Content: are they going to actually make a game like this happen?
Content_zh: 他們真的能讓這樣的遊戲成真嗎？

Content: A game like, that we haven't really been able to make happen for business reasons for like, many, many years.
Content_zh: 這種我們因為商業因素，很多很多年來都做不成的遊戲。

Content: Gilbert: Or we'll just prove all those publishers right.
Content_zh: Gilbert：不然我們就只是證明那些發行商都是對的。

Content: Schafer: Or we'll just prove them all right.
Content_zh: Schafer：不然我們就只是證明他們都是對的。

Content: Schafer: No, I mean nothing I had done before prepared me for what it's like to work with publishers.
Content_zh: Schafer：不，我是說，我以前做過的事，沒有一件能讓我準備好面對跟發行商合作是什麼感覺。

Content: Rice: When you're working with a publisher, a lot of times they get involved with design decisions.
Content_zh: Rice：跟發行商合作時，他們很多時候會介入設計決策。

Content: And you have to make compromises.
Content_zh: 你就得做出妥協。

Content: Gilbert: You know, if a publisher is out there, from the outside kind of poking in and making you do things.
Content_zh: Gilbert：你知道，如果發行商在外面，從外部一直插手，要你做這做那。

Content: You know, that's where I think things can kind of come off the rails.
Content_zh: 我覺得事情就是從那裡開始有點失控。

Content: Schafer: And the way it felt, I don't know if this makes any sense, but my head, like the creative process of making a game was like,
Content_zh: Schafer：那種感覺，我不知道這樣講有沒有道理，但在我腦中，做遊戲的創作過程就像，

Content: you look at a field of floating, multi-colored dots, drifting slowly to the ground.
Content_zh: 你看著一整片漂浮的彩色點點，慢慢飄向地面。

Content: And eventually when they hit the ground, they're going to make like a painting on the floor.
Content_zh: 最後它們落到地上時，會在地板上形成一幅畫。

Content: But you've got to kind of like move them around, give them the right and see.
Content_zh: 但你得稍微移動它們，把它們放到對的位置，看看會怎樣。

Content: And then as they fall, you start to see where you need more color and you move them around and slowly they settle and you just add a sound.
Content_zh: 然後它們落下時，你開始看出哪裡需要更多顏色，就把它們移一移，慢慢讓它們定下來，再加上一點聲音。

Content: And then you're done.
Content_zh: 然後就完成了。

Content: So it'd be like, Tim, what do you do with that green dot?
Content_zh: 所以就會變成，Tim，那個綠點你要怎麼處理？

Content: Phew, I can't tell you.
Content_zh: 呼，我沒辦法告訴你。

Content: You're like, ok, that dot?
Content_zh: 對方就說，好，那個點呢？

Content: Can we just nail that down here.
Content_zh: 我們能不能先把它釘在這裡？

Content: Can we just say what that's going to be?
Content_zh: 能不能先說那會是什麼？

Content: And you're like, no.
Content_zh: 你就說，不行。

Content: So let's just say where all the red dots are going to be, ok.
Content_zh: 那我們先決定所有紅點要在哪裡，好嗎？

Content: Could you just nail all the red dots down?
Content_zh: 你能不能把所有紅點都釘住？

Content: Like, no!
Content_zh: 我就說，不行！

Content: How about if we just start on this edge and we just take all the dots, like this and just nail all those dots.
Content_zh: 那不然我們從這邊開始，把所有像這樣的點都拿來，然後全釘住怎麼樣？

Content: No!
Content_zh: 不行！

Content: Rice: Yeah. The games industry as a whole, I think, is in a really weird spot where it's like we're,
Content_zh: Rice：對。我覺得整個遊戲產業現在處在很奇怪的位置，就像我們，

Content: like the film industry used to be way back in the day.
Content_zh: 有點像很久以前的電影產業。

Content: Where the publishers are running mostly everything.
Content_zh: 當時發行商幾乎掌控一切。

Content: That's kind of how it was for a long time and it's only now that people are starting to like
Content_zh: 長期以來差不多都是那樣，直到現在人們才開始，

Content: seek out the creators of the games.
Content_zh: 去找出遊戲的創作者。

Content: That people who are making the game and try to actually support those people and lift them up a bit.
Content_zh: 找出那些真正做遊戲的人，試著支持他們，把他們往上推一點。

Content: Young Man: Because Tim Schafer has consistently put out quality games.
Content_zh: 年輕男子：因為 Tim Schafer 一直都在推出高品質的遊戲。

Content: Man: Games that have very large and dedicated followings.
Content_zh: 男子：那些遊戲都有非常龐大又死忠的粉絲。

Content: Man: People would probably pay him just to spit on their screen.
Content_zh: 男子：大家可能連他朝螢幕吐口水都願意付錢。

Content: Young Man: And we do appreciate that.
Content_zh: 年輕男子：我們也真的很感謝。

Content: We show our appreciation by doing what we've done.
Content_zh: 我們用我們做的事來表達感謝。

Content: By giving them the funding for this game in eight hours.
Content_zh: 也就是在八小時內給他們這款遊戲的資金。

Content: Rice: The whole like past month has been like a complete blur.
Content_zh: Rice：過去這一個月整個像一團模糊。

Content: It's gone so quick.
Content_zh: 過得太快了。

Content: But, yeah, we were in Vegas for DICE the day that we launched.
Content_zh: 不過，對，我們上線那天人在 Vegas 參加 DICE。

Content: Schafer: And we'd had a hard month, because we had a game cancel.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們那個月很難熬，因為有一款遊戲被取消。

Content: The whole team, we're trying to figure out whether we could swing having this team stay on
Content_zh: 整個團隊都在想，我們能不能撐住，讓這個團隊留下來，

Content: or do we have to let go of twelve people to keep the company afloat?
Content_zh: 還是得裁掉十二個人，才能讓公司撐下去？

Content: We've never laid off anyone for money before.
Content_zh: 我們以前從來沒有因為錢裁過人。

Content: So we were, and even besides the financial concerns, it was just such a, it doesn't feel like you're winning when you have a game canceled.
Content_zh: 所以我們當時，除了財務上的擔憂之外，遊戲被取消真的讓人覺得自己一點都不像贏家。

Content: You feel like it's a big, you know, you've lost.
Content_zh: 你會覺得那是一場很大的，怎麼說，你輸了。

Content: Schafer: I'm about to launch our first Kickstarter project.
Content_zh: Schafer：我要推出我們第一個 Kickstarter 專案了。

Content: Rice: It kind of just all came together right when we were out there and we had a bit of time before a meeting and we're like,
Content_zh: Rice：事情剛好就在我們人在那邊、會議前有點時間時湊在一起，我們就想，

Content: we could launch this today.
Content_zh: 今天就可以上線。

Content: Schafer: Yeah, I did have to like decide, well, we'll launch our Kickstarter.
Content_zh: Schafer：對，我確實得決定，好吧，我們要上線 Kickstarter。

Content: That, for time, we're like I guess $200,000.
Content_zh: 那時候我們想，大概二十萬美元吧。

Content: We could make something for $200,000.
Content_zh: 我們可以用二十萬美元做出點東西。

Content: And I had to accept it, I might only get $200,000, right?
Content_zh: 而我得接受一件事：也許我只會拿到二十萬美元，對吧？

Content: And then when I was nervous about it, so I upped it to three, right.
Content_zh: 後來我有點緊張，所以把目標拉到三十萬，對。

Content: And so it was like, yeah, we can make something for $300,000.
Content_zh: 所以就變成，對，我們可以用三十萬美元做出東西。

Content: Because we could.
Content_zh: 因為我們做得到。

Content: You know, we'd been talking about iPhone games recently and it's like three people for six months or something like that.
Content_zh: 你知道，我們最近一直在聊 iPhone 遊戲，像是三個人做六個月之類的。

Content: Yeah, we could make something with three people and six months.
Content_zh: 對，三個人、六個月，我們可以做出點東西。

Content: It would look like a flash game or it'd look like an iPhone game, but it would technically be a game.
Content_zh: 它看起來會像 Flash 遊戲，或像 iPhone 遊戲，但技術上仍然是一款遊戲。

Content: By all scientific definitions.
Content_zh: 按照所有科學定義來說。

Content: Schafer: I'm nervous.
Content_zh: Schafer：我好緊張。

Content: Launch.
Content_zh: 上線。

Content: Review, i'll have to review it.
Content_zh: 審核，我得先審核一下。

Content: Rice: Oh, man. That was such a letdown!
Content_zh: Rice：喔，天啊。這也太掃興了！

Content: Schafer: OK. No, no, that's just fine print.
Content_zh: Schafer：好。沒事，沒事，只是小字條款。

Content: It's just that I never read these things.
Content_zh: 只是我從來不看這些東西。

Content: Should I do it?
Content_zh: 我要按嗎？

Content: Rice: Yeah, do it.
Content_zh: Rice：對，按吧。

Content: Schafer: It couldn't hurt, right?
Content_zh: Schafer：應該不會怎樣吧？

Content: Rice: It could, but we're not going to let it.
Content_zh: Rice：可能會，但我們不會讓它出事。

Content: Schafer: Whoo!
Content_zh: Schafer：嗚！

Content: Rice: I remember like as soon as we hit it up and we refresh, it was like at $30.
Content_zh: Rice：我記得我們一按下去、刷新頁面，就已經有 30 美元了。

Content: We're like, holy crap! There's already $30.
Content_zh: 我們就想，哇靠！已經有 30 美元了。

Content: Schafer: I think I'm going to look at how much, I don't care about money.
Content_zh: Schafer：我想看一下有多少，我才不在乎錢。

Content: I'm not going to look. I'm going to look, just one second.
Content_zh: 我不看。我要看一下，等我一秒。

Content: Rice: What are we at?
Content_zh: Rice：現在到多少了？

Content: OK, so we posted this, what? Two hours ago, right?
Content_zh: 好，所以我們發布這個，多久？兩小時前，對吧？

Content: Schafer: Two hours ago.
Content_zh: Schafer：兩小時前。

Content: We launched our Kickstarter project two hours ago.
Content_zh: 我們兩小時前上線了 Kickstarter 專案。

Content: A hundred and six, I can't count so high.
Content_zh: 十萬六，我數不了那麼高。

Content: It's such a high number, that's crazy.
Content_zh: 數字太高了，太瘋狂了。

Content: It's too much money.
Content_zh: 錢太多了。

Content: Rice: Yeah. So when we hit a million, that was definitely like, that was the day after.
Content_zh: Rice：對。所以我們破一百萬時，那絕對是，應該是隔天。

Content: One day after we launched, right?
Content_zh: 上線後一天，對吧？

Content: Rice: What are we at?
Content_zh: Rice：現在多少了？

Content: Schafer: I want to just say I love you guys and not for the money.
Content_zh: Schafer：我只想說我愛你們，而且不是為了錢。

Content: Offscreen: 999,700!
Muir: Oh my god, they're counting down to a refresh.
Content_zh: Muir：天啊，他們在倒數刷新。

Content: Muir: Oh my God! It's so exciting.
Content_zh: Muir：天啊！太刺激了。

Content: Rice: What's the number?
Content_zh: Rice：數字是多少？

Content: Offscreen: Refresh! Refresh!
Content_zh: 畫外音：刷新！刷新！

Content: Min: The internet has crashed.
Content_zh: Min：網路掛了。

Content: No!
Content_zh: 不要！

Content: Min: The internet has crashed.
Content_zh: Min：網路掛了。

Content: Rice: And what? No!
Content_zh: Rice：什麼？不要！

Content: Schafer: Where's my phone?
Content_zh: Schafer：我的手機在哪？

Content: Muir: The Internet's crashed.
Content_zh: Muir：網路掛了。

Content: [Offscreen Yelling] [Distortion]
Content_zh: [畫外叫喊聲] [失真聲]

Content: I think they all died. They're dying!
Content_zh: 我覺得他們都死了。他們快死了！

Content: Are you guys ok? What happened?
Content_zh: 你們還好嗎？發生什麼事？

Content: Oh, they're being crushed. It's horrible.
Content_zh: 喔，他們被壓扁了。太可怕了。

Content: Muir: Oh, no! No!
Content_zh: Muir：喔，不！不要！

Content: What happened?
Content_zh: 發生什麼事？

Content: [Distortion] Muir: It's a million dollars!
Content_zh: [失真聲] Muir：一百萬美元！

Content: Rice: Is it?
Content_zh: Rice：真的嗎？

Content: Muir: That's what a million dollars sounds like.
Content_zh: Muir：一百萬美元聽起來就是這樣。

Content: Offscreen: What do you mean it crashed?
Content_zh: 畫外音：你說掛了是什麼意思？

Content: Offscreen: Kickstarter crashed.
Content_zh: 畫外音：Kickstarter 掛了。

Content: Muir: Kickstarter crashed!
Content_zh: Muir：Kickstarter 掛了！

Content: Schafer: No.
Content_zh: Schafer：不要。

Content: [Offscreen yelling] Rice: Yeah, that was definitely one of the high points,
Content_zh: [畫外叫喊聲] Rice：對，那絕對是最高點之一，

Content: where it was like, holy crap -- This is a huge thing.
Content_zh: 當時就覺得，哇靠，這是件大事。

Content: Muir: Whoo! A million in 24 hours.
Content_zh: Muir：嗚！24 小時內一百萬。

Content: Tim Schafer, you just made a million dollars in 24 hours!
Content_zh: Tim Schafer，你剛在 24 小時內賺到一百萬美元！

Content: What do you, what are you doing?
Content_zh: 你要，你現在要做什麼？

Content: Schafer: Yeah! I got to make a game.
Content_zh: Schafer：對！我得做遊戲了。

Content: Newscaster: Over the last few days, Tim Schafer from Double Fine took crowd funding site, Kickstarter to fund his next game.
Content_zh: 新聞主播：過去幾天，Double Fine 的 Tim Schafer 利用群眾募資網站 Kickstarter 為他的下一款遊戲籌資。

Content: Schafer: It feels like a happening. You know what I mean?
Content_zh: Schafer：感覺像是一場現象。你懂我的意思嗎？

Content: Like it feels like an event.
Content_zh: 就像一件大事。

Content: Newscaster: They're raising money at a clip of a thousand dollars per minute.
Content_zh: 新聞主播：他們正以每分鐘一千美元的速度籌款。

Content: It's incredible.
Content_zh: 這太不可思議了。

Content: Host: One question that is on everybody's mind.
Content_zh: 主持人：有個大家都很好奇的問題。

Content: You get the million dollars.
Content_zh: 你們拿到一百萬美元。

Content: Did you immediately withdraw it all?
Content_zh: 你們有馬上全部領出來嗎？

Content: Put it into a vault and then dive into it like Scrooge McDuck style?
Content_zh: 放進金庫裡，然後像 Scrooge McDuck 一樣跳進去游泳？

Content: Schafer: Then during Kickstarter, it made it, I don't know what it was,
Content_zh: Schafer：然後 Kickstarter 期間，它讓，嗯，我不知道那是什麼，

Content: but it made everyone just take that big basket of money and shake it out over our heads.
Content_zh: 但它讓大家拿起那一大籃錢，往我們頭上倒下來。

Content: It really was like the end of "It's a Wonderful Life".
Content_zh: 真的很像《It's a Wonderful Life》的結尾。

Content: [Music] Offscreen: So excited.
Content_zh: [音樂] 畫外音：太興奮了。

Content: Offscreen: Thumbs up?
Content_zh: 畫外音：比讚嗎？

Content: Man: Thumbs up, yes.
Content_zh: 男子：比讚，對。

Content: That was pretty amazing.
Content_zh: 真的很驚人。

Content: I told you, adventure games are not dead!
Content_zh: 我就說吧，冒險遊戲還沒死！

Content: The thing that I noticed was that it was just such a morale boost to the whole team.
Content_zh: 我注意到的是，這對整個團隊真的是很大的士氣提升。

Content: And you know, I think it just felt like a lot of goodwill and love coming towards the company and everyone got super excited about it.
Content_zh: 你知道，我覺得那就像有很多善意和愛湧向公司，大家都超興奮。

Content: And because it was like this feeling of like, we knew those people were there, we knew they were.
Content_zh: 因為那種感覺就像，我們知道那些人存在，我們知道他們在那裡。

Content: But, because sometimes they don't show up when you make a game.
Content_zh: 可是有時候你做遊戲時，他們不一定會出現。

Content: You know, you make a game, you release it, you know, like I thought it was going to sell a lot and it didn't.
Content_zh: 你知道，你做了一款遊戲，發行了，原本以為會賣很多，結果沒有。

Content: You're like, where are all those people that it feels like, there's all this goodwill out there.
Content_zh: 你就會想，那些人都去哪了？明明感覺外面有這麼多善意。

Content: Man #1: It's two million.
Content_zh: 男子 #1：兩百萬了。

Content: Man #2: One of us has to stop shaving.
Content_zh: 男子 #2：我們其中一個人得停止刮毛。

Content: Man #2: He has to shave my scrotum if it gets to two million.
Content_zh: 男子 #2：如果到兩百萬，他就得幫我刮陰囊。

Content: Man #1: Only if he agrees to do it on camera.
Content_zh: 男子 #1：除非他同意在鏡頭前刮。

Content: Offscreen: Congratulations.
Content_zh: 畫外音：恭喜。

Content: Schafer: It had been a long time since we had experience where it felt like we were the winners.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們已經很久沒有這種覺得自己是贏家的經驗了。

Content: We were at DICE and I was walking through DICE, feeling like a winner, which is really kind of like,
Content_zh: 我們當時在 DICE，我走在 DICE 會場裡，覺得自己像個贏家，那真的有點像，

Content: not that I feel like a loser, but usually feel like the alternative, alternative to money.
Content_zh: 不是說我覺得自己像輸家，但平常比較像替代方案，錢以外的替代方案。

Content: I mean i'm proud of all our games and I wouldn't switch them with anyone else's games,
Content_zh: 我是說，我為我們所有遊戲感到驕傲，也不會拿它們去換任何人的遊戲，

Content: but usually walking in a convention like that, you're talking to the, you know, the people who made Uncharted
Content_zh: 但平常走在那種展會裡，你會跟那些，你知道，做《Uncharted》的人聊天，

Content: and they're, they're really nice and you're like, ah.
Content_zh: 他們真的都很好，你就會想，啊。

Content: Congratulations on the game.
Content_zh: 恭喜你們的遊戲。

Content: Did really well for you.
Content_zh: 賣得真好。

Content: And like, you don't often feel like, you feel like they're like the family member who's like still living at home.
Content_zh: 你不常有那種感覺，你會覺得自己像家裡那個還住在家裡的親戚。

Content: You know, like yeah.
Content_zh: 你知道，就像，對。

Content: They're all really nice to us, but you know, we haven't, it's different when you make a bunch of money.
Content_zh: 他們對我們都很好，但你知道，我們沒有，賺到一大筆錢時感覺就是不一樣。

Content: Schafer: And we raised 1.7 million dollars on Kickstarter.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們在 Kickstarter 上籌到了 170 萬美元。

Content: [Applause] Schafer: Is that auto refreshing?
Content_zh: [掌聲] Schafer：那是自動刷新嗎？

Content: Everyone: Yes.
Content_zh: 眾人：對。

Content: Schafer: We were in the hotel room and I was listening on the Skype, on the iPad
Content_zh: Schafer：我們在飯店房間，我用 iPad 上的 Skype 聽著，

Content: and you just remember like, click, click, aha, aha, then all of a sudden, whooo!
Content_zh: 你只記得像是，喀、喀，啊哈、啊哈，然後突然，嗚！

Content: Schafer: Distortion sound as everyone lost their shit.
Content_zh: Schafer：大家嗨到失控時的失真聲。

Content: And right now we're at almost 2.5 million dollars.
Content_zh: 現在我們已經快到 250 萬美元了。

Content: and we have six more days to go.
Content_zh: 而且還有六天。

Content: Rice, Offscreen: Everything moved really quickly and I actually didn't really have a chance
Content_zh: Rice，畫外音：一切都進展得很快，我其實一直沒有機會

Content: to like let everything sit in until yesterday, when it finished.
Content_zh: 好好消化這一切，直到昨天專案結束。

Content: And I was like, finally it like hit me and it was like, holy crap.
Content_zh: 然後我才終於有感覺，心想，哇靠。

Content: We did something really crazy here.
Content_zh: 我們真的做了一件很瘋狂的事。

Content: Schafer: 2 minutes!
Content_zh: Schafer：兩分鐘！

Content: [Crowd shouting and cheering] So like we cleared in advance
Content_zh: [群眾叫喊歡呼] 所以我們事先講好了，

Content: I'm going to shake this up and I'm going to spray it all over your head.
Content_zh: 我要把這個搖一搖，然後噴滿你的頭。

Content: Why is that...
Content_zh: 那為什麼……

Content: Ron!
Content_zh: Ron！

Content: My God, that's terrible for your teeth.
Content_zh: 天啊，那對你的牙齒很傷。

Content: Offscreen: Two minutes!
Content_zh: 畫外音：兩分鐘！

Content: Two minutes.
Content_zh: 兩分鐘。

Content: [Pop] Offscreen: Premature! Premature!
Content_zh: [砰] 畫外音：太早了！太早了！

Content: Gilbert: There's still a part of my skeptical pessimistic brain that says, well, you can never do this again.
Content_zh: Gilbert：我那顆多疑又悲觀的腦袋裡，還是有一部分在說，好吧，你再也不可能做到第二次了。

Content: That this was really kind of this one time perfect storm thing.
Content_zh: 這真的有點像是一次性的天時地利人和。

Content: But I don't know. I mean, maybe not.
Content_zh: 但我不知道。我是說，也許不一定。

Content: [Music] [Cheering]
Content_zh: [音樂] [歡呼聲]

Content: I've been so happy about that ever since Kickstarter started,
Content_zh: 從 Kickstarter 開始之後，我一直很開心，

Content: just thinking about how, I still have to answer to people.
Content_zh: 因為想到，我還是得對某些人負責。

Content: Like people funded the game, you know.
Content_zh: 有人出錢支持這款遊戲，你知道。

Content: But they're exactly the people I want to answer to.
Content_zh: 但他們正是我想負責的人。

Content: Those are the people I'm making dots for, right?
Content_zh: 他們就是我在做那些點點時想著的人，對吧？

Content: And all those things like, and so it's like, it's just this really liberating,
Content_zh: 還有那些事情，所以這真的很讓人解放，

Content: freeing thing to just think that I'm just going to have to worry about how good the game is, almost nothing else.
Content_zh: 想到我只需要擔心遊戲夠不夠好，幾乎不用擔心別的事，感覺很自由。

Content: [Music] Schafer: I love you guys!
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：我愛你們！

Content: It has been an amazing experience. So much love.
Content_zh: 這真的是很棒的經驗。滿滿的愛。

Content: We know there's not, it's not all about us and Double Fine, but about you, the fans and what you guys want to say
Content_zh: 我們知道，這不只是關於我們和 Double Fine，而是關於你們，粉絲們，還有你們想表達什麼，

Content: and do with your money and choose to do.
Content_zh: 想用自己的錢做什麼、選擇做什麼。

Content: And make things happen for yourself.
Content_zh: 為自己促成想看到的事。

Content: I mean, I don't want to make a huge speech here, because I'll be all choked up.
Content_zh: 我是說，我不想在這裡講一大段，因為我會哽咽。

Content: Gilbert: Too late.
Content_zh: Gilbert：太晚了。

Content: Schafer: But I just, I don't want to say this is the end of the, you know, the whole game industry as we know it.
Content_zh: Schafer：但我只是，我不想說這代表我們所知道的整個遊戲產業要結束了。

Content: It's not, it's not.
Content_zh: 不是，不是。

Content: And it's not, again it's not like we're total replacement of all publishers and publishing games.
Content_zh: 而且再說一次，這也不是說我們會完全取代所有發行商和遊戲發行。

Content: There's still going to be, there still might be a few games made by publishers after today.
Content_zh: 今天之後，發行商大概還是會做幾款遊戲。

Content: Just a couple games.
Content_zh: 就幾款而已。

Content: But it does mean if you've ever been told that your part of a niche market, if you've ever like, if you've ever like,
Content_zh: 但這代表，如果你曾經被告知自己屬於小眾市場，如果你曾經，像是，

Content: when you're a kid and you had your favorite TV show cancelled or you hear about your favorite band
Content_zh: 小時候最愛的電視節目被取消，或聽說最愛的樂團

Content: being dropped by their label for not selling enough and you've just been like why does a big company get to choose
Content_zh: 因為賣得不夠好而被唱片公司放棄，你會想，為什麼大公司可以決定

Content: what music I listen to or what movies I watch or what TV shows I watch or what game I get to play,
Content_zh: 我要聽什麼音樂、看什麼電影、看什麼電視節目，或玩什麼遊戲，

Content: now you know that they can't do that anymore.
Content_zh: 現在你知道，他們不能再那樣做了。

Content: You can choose. So thanks everybody.
Content_zh: 你可以自己選。所以謝謝大家。

Content: [Cheering] Thank you to the team!
Content_zh: [歡呼聲] 謝謝團隊！

Content: Everybody!
Content_zh: 謝謝大家！

Content: Thanks to my parents for backing the project and everybody else's parents who backed the project.
Content_zh: 謝謝我的父母贊助這個專案，也謝謝所有其他贊助專案的父母。

Content: Does anybody else want to thank anybody?
Content_zh: 還有人想謝謝誰嗎？

Content: Kipnis: Thank you Tim!
Content_zh: Kipnis：謝謝你，Tim！

Content: Thank you!
Content_zh: 謝謝！

Content: Thanks everyone and now we dance.
Content_zh: 謝謝大家，現在我們來跳舞。

Content: [Cheering] Ron's dancing!
Content_zh: [歡呼聲] Ron 在跳舞！

Content: [Music]
Content_zh: [音樂]
